objective: Launch-ready product hardening with continuous source-backed AutoResearch A/B loops, GitHub project comparison, direct app-click QA, commit/push evidence, and no global stop until the user explicitly stops.

global_completion_policy.reason: The user requested a continuous self-improvement loop until an explicit stop. Current cycles can be complete, but the global objective remains active.

criteria.requirement: Product launch hardening has a clean-worktree all-scope proof.
GitHub-related similar projects are tracked and mapped to local evidence.
The active launch proof is checked against the current remote branch so product-code changes after the proof invalidate cycle readiness.
GitHub-related modernization sources have a live repository metadata freshness snapshot.
The GitHub source freshness snapshot has a recent generated_at timestamp and passing status so old external-source evidence cannot satisfy the launch cycle indefinitely.
GitHub source freshness fails when a tracked repository is archived, disabled, or missing core repository metadata.
GitHub source freshness marks unauthenticated API rate-limit refreshes as partial token-required failures and keeps them separate from complete source snapshots.
AutoResearch self-improvement skill exists and enforces A/B, evidence, commit/push, and completion-audit behavior.
Direct browser/app-click QA was performed on launch-critical user surfaces.
Launch-critical app/browser-smoke paths have not changed after the direct browser QA proof baseline.

must_contain: has protected paths changed after proof commit

criteria.requirement: Accepted variants are recorded, committed, and pushed to the active branch.
The local push gate protects the MCP runtime and completion-critical smoke tests.
The dev-server MCP runtime has a repo-owned subprocess smoke that validates real JSON-RPC stdio behavior.
Repo-local stdio MCP services prove runtime initialize and tools/list readiness against the service manifest.
The MCP OTLP smoke export has an executable, source-backed collector handoff check without claiming live collector shipping.
Declared agent workflow quality gates can be executed through existing project CLIs without claiming full autonomous orchestration.
Side-effecting workflow gates are skipped by default in execute mode unless an operator explicitly allows them.
All declared launch workflows can be verified through a safe deterministic gate matrix.
The launch workflow matrix reuses duplicate deterministic gate results without hiding per-workflow evidence.
Transient AutoResearch/browser-smoke artifacts under var stay local-only so repeated self-improvement cycles do not pollute commit and push decisions.
Unfinished or credential-bound work is explicitly tracked instead of hidden behind green tests.
Credential-gated and operator-owned launch boundaries are machine-readable and executable without exposing secret values.
Credential-gated operator actions have a redacted handoff package with env names, verification commands, and no secret values.

must_contain: Fill values locally; do not commit populated secrets.

criteria.requirement: Credential-gated operator commands can be planned and safely executed only after required env is present.
The original user prompt is mapped to concrete artifacts, completion criteria, and explicit blockers.
Checked-in GitHub source freshness snapshot matches the current modernization source manifest and renderer output.

must_contain: GitHub Source Snapshot Manifest Drift Guard

criteria.requirement: Checked-in GitHub modernization radar report matches the current modernization source manifest and renderer output.

must_contain: Keep the default smoke gate deterministic and offline

criteria.requirement: Checked-in AutoResearch objective coverage artifacts match the current requirements manifest and renderer output.
Checked-in AutoResearch completion audit summary artifacts match the current completion contract and renderer output.
Credential-gated actions are not claimed complete without real user credentials.
criteria.blocker: Canva OAuth and live OpenAPI execution require real user login/consent and proxy authentication decisions.
